item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really love the testing logs in this one - you've shown the investigative process by which The Foundation figured out this thing's abilities, and made it feel like genuine scientific enquiry, without it being over-long or tedious. +1

item.title: Very Creative
item.content:encoded: Very Original, haven't seen anything like it. Happy to see new SCP's doing well!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even corporations that can sell you personal demons to use will still try to rip you off with the worst devils they can find.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, there it is then

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Step 2: Drop three drops of our imitation blood into the powder, and use it to write your name.

Since the whole process was bundled into the powder, writing your name in the powder is essentially signing the contract.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When these instructions are followed, the powder around the area where the subject's name was written will begin to steam

As it is, prrretty sure I'm not seeing any mention of name-writing before this point. Or of contracts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As in Prometheus and Bob, from the old KaBlam! cartoons?

… I'll get me coat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks!

Yeah, I wrote it as this was all taking place during one test, or relevant information spliced together from more than one. It didn't really feel relevant to mention either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The act that it's trying to destroy the Is to make way for the Was is just an act. This is a cheap knockoff demon, so it just behaves like a stereotypical demon. Basically interfacing with it would be like talking to occult!Cleverbot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm afraid this isn't clicking with me. There are certain elements of genius here, but tonally I think it's incredibly inconsistent. It goes from 'anomalous bastardization of shite consumer lifehacks' to 'gonzo demon servant what is power for the lols' to 'dark force bound to enter our dimension and eradicate the Is to make way for the Was.' I really don't have anything wrong with any of those things, but they're clustered here in such a way that they counteract their individual potentials. What could be funny is offset and made awkward by the 'terrifying eldritch talk', and what could be scary is negated by the 'Just Add [insert abnormal ingredient]' If the article could choose an angle to go down, I can see it working far better than it already does; as it stands, however, I feel like I need to downvote. There's definitely something here, but it's obfuscated by a narrative that's going in several different thematic directions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I'm sorry, my responses are limited. Please try another question."
(from "I, Robot", the movie)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Asmodeus Labs seems like it could be a spinoff of Prometheus Labs applied goetics program after the breakup.

Also Bob was the name of the "Fire Walk With Me" demon in Twin Peaks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fantastic. This guy is like the magic version of Sea-Monkeys, wherein a whole lot is promised on the packaging, but in reality, ehhhhh, it's okay, I guess. :)

"and succ on this" made me laugh quite a bit, thank you for that.

+1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
S U C C

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a lot of fun. I can't believe I'm upvoting a skip with the word 'succ' in it, but that's definitely part of the reason I'm upvoting. And I love that they're like, hey, real blood works too, if that's what does it for ya!

I do have to say, it seems like the testing logs might all be from the same test, rather than individual activations. I mean, it lasts for two hours, right? All that stuff could happen in two hours.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So I have this thing, where Wondertainment-like-companies drive me nuts.

That being said, this is done very well, and is kind of cute in the "Cheap-plastic-knockoff" demon summoning kinda way.

+1

You set up family friendly demon summoning, I say to myself "yeah this doesn't end well", and then in a freakin' coup you literally give me family friendly demon summoning! Fun to read, amazingly paced for the payoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's always lovely to see a draft I helped with be so successful (: Congratulations! I always liked the idea :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This subverted my expectations really well - by setting up 'thing that summons demons' in a setting where that's totally believable, I then felt properly disappointed and tricked at the slow realisation that it's just a cheap perception trick. This nicely emulates the real-life experience of buying crappy knockoff toys and tricks as a kid.

There are some definite tonal issues here (your use of 'succ' feels particularly forced in context), but this is excellently structured.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh, never knew that article existed.
Also I have no idea what the Laundry Files are lol but as long as it made you laugh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think Asmodeus could've been folded in under Prometheus, but apart from that quibble I giggled like a three year old at the "Bob" reference. If it's related to the Laundry Files you get bonus points.

item.title: Re: +1
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I've got no real plans at the moment to reuse Asmodeus Labs, but come to think of it, there's a surprising shortage of GoIs dedicated to magic. Well, that call it that at least.

This is a fairly simple idea that's done quite well! The fact that -1 can only alter the perception of its summoner I find really interesting. Almost makes it feel like a 'knockoff' sort of thing; like, acts like a real demon, but isn't as cool. There's some pretty good GoI potential here. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't get ninja'd this time, mwahaha. Thanks to shaggydredlocks, Railgun45, Zachary Maxwell, and MrWrong for giving me feedback on this one!
Yeah I used 'succ' in an SCP, you got a problem with that? kappa
